mission.title: Install and Configure ClickHouse
mission.description: ClickHouse is a real-time analytics database management system designed for generating analytical data reports efficiently. It is ideal for scenarios requiring high-speed data processing and real-time analytics. Key trade-offs include the need for careful schema design and potential complexity in managing distributed setups.

steps.title: Add the ClickHouse Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ClickHouse Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add clickhouse https://clickhouse.com/helm/charts

```

> This command sets up the repository for installing ClickHouse.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for ClickHouse to function properly in Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/master/helm/clickhouse/templates/crds.yaml

```

> This command ensures that all necessary CRDs are created.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs ClickHouse into the specified namespace using the Helm chart.

```bash

helm install clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --create-namespace --version 26.2.4.23-stable

```

> This command deploys ClickHouse with the specified version.

steps.title: Create an example ClickHouse table
steps.description:
This step creates a sample table in ClickHouse to verify that the installation was successful.

```bash

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse -l app=clickhouse -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- clickhouse-client --query 'CREATE TABLE test (id UInt32, name String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;'

```

> This command creates a simple table for testing.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse -l app=clickhouse -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- clickhouse-client --query 'CREATE TABLE test (id UInt32, name String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;'

steps.title: Verify ClickHouse is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the ClickHouse pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse

```

> You should see the ClickHouse pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ClickHouse
uninstall.description:
This step removes ClickHouse from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in ClickHouse.

```bash

helm uninstall clickhouse --namespace clickhouse

kubectl delete namespace clickhouse

```

> This command removes the ClickHouse release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ClickHouse
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade ClickHouse to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse -l app=clickhouse -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- clickhouse-client --query 'BACKUP TABLE test TO 'backup_location';'

helm upgrade clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --version 26.2.4.23-stable

kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse

```

> Backup your data before proceeding.
> This command upgrades ClickHouse to the specified version.
> Verify that the ClickHouse pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse -l app=clickhouse -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- clickhouse-client --query 'BACKUP TABLE test TO 'backup_location';'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cannot connect to ClickHouse server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse server may not be running or is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the ClickHouse pods with 'kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse' and ensure they are in Running state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Table test does not exist'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified table may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Re-run the table creation command and check for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install ClickHouse: chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse Helm repository may not be added correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-add the Helm repository using 'helm repo add clickhouse https://clickhouse.com/helm/charts'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources available for ClickHouse.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster meets the minimum resource requirements (2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new MergeTree features and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the ClickHouse pods running with 'kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse' showing the status as Running.
codeSnippets: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse -l app=clickhouse -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- clickhouse-client --query 'CREATE TABLE test (id UInt32, name String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;'

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up with sufficient resources.
